title: Python Data Structures for Data Science
description: A hierarchical diagram showing the primary data structures used in Python for data science, categorized by their core functionality and common use cases.
relationships: Lists and Tuples are ordered sequences, with Lists being mutable and Tuples immutable
Dictionaries and Sets are unordered collections, with Dictionaries storing key-value pairs and Sets storing unique elements
NumPy Arrays and Pandas DataFrames/Series are specialized structures built on top of core Python for efficient numerical and tabular data manipulation
